Preconditions: Bastion installed; service account created; port 636 open
Test Steps: 1. From Bastion CLI run: ldapsearch -H ldaps://[DC-IP]:636 -D [service-acct] -w [password] -b [base-dn] 2. Verify results return
Expected Result: User objects returned from LDAP query without error

Preconditions: FortiAuthenticator installed; RADIUS client configured; test user with token enrolled
Test Steps: 1. From Bastion: radtest [username] [password+otp] [FA-IP] 1812 [shared-secret] 2. Verify Access-Accept response
Expected Result: Access-Accept received for valid credentials + correct OTP

Preconditions: Target onboarded; firewall rules applied; user account in vault
Test Steps: 1. Log in to Bastion as test user 2. Select SSH target 3. Click Connect 4. Verify terminal opens
Expected Result: Terminal session opens; session appears in Bastion session list

Preconditions: Target onboarded; firewall rules applied; user account in vault
Test Steps: 1. Log in to Bastion as test user 2. Select RDP target 3. Click Connect 4. Verify RDP session opens
Expected Result: RDP session opens; session appears in Bastion session list

Preconditions: Telnet target onboarded; serial-to-IP converter reachable
Test Steps: 1. Log in to Bastion as test user 2. Select Telnet target 3. Click Connect 4. Verify Telnet session opens
Expected Result: Telnet session opens; session appears in Bastion session list

Preconditions: AD integration configured; test user in correct AD group
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to Bastion login page 2. Enter valid AD username and password 3. Complete MFA 4. Verify login succeeds
Expected Result: Login succeeds; user sees their approved target list

Preconditions: Same as T-P2-04
Test Steps: 1. Enter valid AD username with wrong password 2. Attempt login
Expected Result: Login rejected with authentication error; no access granted

Preconditions: MFA configured; test user token enrolled
Test Steps: 1. Log in with valid AD credentials 2. Enter current TOTP code from token 3. Complete login
Expected Result: Login succeeds

Preconditions: Same as T-P2-06
Test Steps: 1. Log in with valid AD credentials 2. Enter an incorrect TOTP code
Expected Result: Login rejected; MFA failure recorded in FortiAuthenticator log

Preconditions: SSH session active
Test Steps: 1. Open SSH session to test target 2. Run several commands 3. Close session 4. Open Bastion audit log 5. Find and replay the session
Expected Result: Session replay shows all commands entered; timestamps correct

Preconditions: RDP session active
Test Steps: 1. Open RDP session 2. Open Notepad; type test text 3. Close session 4. Replay recording in Bastion
Expected Result: Replay shows RDP session video; text typed in Notepad visible

Preconditions: SIEM integration configured
Test Steps: 1. Open and close a test session 2. Check SIEM within 5 minutes for session open and session close events
Expected Result: Events appear in SIEM with correct user identity and target

Preconditions: Both nodes running; test session open on active node
Test Steps: 1. Open a session through Bastion VIP 2. Stop the active Bastion node (systemctl stop wallix-bastion) 3. Verify VIP migrates to passive node 4. Verify new sessions can be opened
Expected Result: VIP migrates within 30 seconds; new sessions open successfully

Preconditions: Both HAProxy nodes running
Test Steps: 1. Stop HAProxy on the active node 2. Verify VIP migrates to standby HAProxy 3. Open a session through the VIP
Expected Result: VIP migrates within 10 seconds; sessions open through standby HAProxy

Preconditions: Rotation policy configured; target reachable from Bastion
Test Steps: 1. Trigger manual rotation on test Linux account 2. Verify rotation status shows Success 3. Attempt SSH session using old password 4. Verify Bastion session using injected (new) credential works
Expected Result: Rotation succeeds; old password rejected on direct login; Bastion session opens with new credential

Preconditions: JIT target configured; approver account available
Test Steps: 1. Log in as requester; submit JIT access request 2. Log in as approver; approve the request 3. Requester opens approved session 4. Session auto-closes at window end 5. Review audit log
Expected Result: Request created; approval notification sent; session opens; auto-close works; audit record complete

Test Name: User cannot access targets outside their role
Preconditions: Two user accounts with different roles configured
Test Steps: 1. Log in as User A (Operator role) 2. Attempt to access a target assigned only to Admin role
Expected Result: Target not visible or access denied

Preconditions: Break-glass credentials in sealed envelope
Test Steps: 1. Take both Bastion nodes offline 2. Open sealed envelope; extract credentials 3. Connect directly to a target using break-glass account 4. Document the access 5. Re-seal credentials
Expected Result: Direct access achieved within 5 minutes; connection documented

Test Name: Full user acceptance test — IT admin workflow
Preconditions: All Phase 2 configuration complete; IT admin trained
Test Steps: 1. IT admin logs in, opens SSH and RDP sessions, reviews own session recordings, adds a test device, adds a test user
Expected Result: All tasks completed without consultant assistance

Test Name: Full user acceptance test — operator workflow
Preconditions: Operator trained; hardware token activated
Test Steps: 1. Operator logs in with hardware TOTP 2. Opens approved RDP session 3. Works normally for 10 minutes 4. Closes session
Expected Result: Login succeeds; session opens; no latency complaints; session closes cleanly
